text: The surgeon views the distal rectal cuff through the anal port following transanal total mesorectal excision. A purse string suture is placed around the circumference of the rectal cuff using either standard surgical instruments or laparoscopic instruments, with the suture ends left untied.

The surgeon inserts a ten French ReadyVac drain through the center of the purse string suture on the distal rectal cuff, advancing it into the abdominal cavity. Once visible within the cavity, the purse string is knotted tightly around the drain. The spindle of a twenty-eight millimeter circular stapler is fed into the distal end of the drain, guiding it through the purse string into the abdominal cavity.

The laparoscopic operator visualizes the spindle of the circular stapler fully within the abdominal cavity before removing the drain from the spindle. The stapler is positioned accurately through the purse string, and the anal port is removed prior to closing the stapler to form the stapled anastomosis.
